Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Mar-27-2018 Text Ruling

Theodore Chase, Esq. of The Goldman Law Firm, T:415.435.5500, for defendants/moving party;

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS SIAMAK JAFARI and JAFARI, DMD, INC.'s Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings as to all claims except as to the breach of contract and breach of implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing causes of action.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Preclude Evidence Supporting Plaintiffs Quantum Meruit Claim Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 454
Off Calendar Mar-27-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark Freeman, Esq. of Venardi Zurada LLP, Tel. No. 925.937.3900, appearing for the Plaintiff Ron Indech;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Plaintiff's counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by Judge Pro Tem Steven Stein.

Matter called, no argument held.

Ruling: There being no appearance by any other party, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Defendants Siamak Jafari, DMD, and Jarfari, DMD, Inc.'s Motion To Preclude Evidence Supporting Plaintiffs Quantum Meruit Claim Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure as follows: Defendant's motion to preclude evidence due to failure to respond to a CCP 454 Demand for a Bill of Particulars is denied. The plaintiff did not fail to timely respond to the demand. The initial response served was an objection and referencing of other discovery responses. No motion to compel was filed. Shortly after the filing this motion plaintiff served a more extensive Bill of Particulars response which defendant in its Reply urges is inadequate. The adequacy of the recently served second response is not before the Court and is a matter reserved to the trial judge. Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)

Motion To Quash, Or Alternatively, For A Protective Order
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Mark L. Venardi, Esq. of Venardi Zurada LLP, Tel. No. 925.937.3900, appearing by CourtCall for the Plaintiff Ron Indech;
Theodore Chase, Esq. of The Goldman Law Firm, Tel. No. 415.435.5500, appearing for the Defendant Jafari, DMD, Inc. Dba Noe Valley Smiles and Braces.

Both Counsel stipulate to the matter being heard by Judge Pro Tem Thomas A. Cohen.

Matter called, argument held.

Ruling: At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on Plaintiff Ron Indech's Motion To Quash, Or Alternatively, For A Protective Order as follows: The motion is denied without prejudice to the custodian of records right to raise any objections to the subpoena including, but not limited to, the burden of providing responsive documents. The Court notes that while tax returns are subject to privacy protections, with exceptions, the W-2s and 1099s requested are not tax returns per se. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas A. Cohen, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Matter Not Reported. =(302/JPT/HEK)
